intro: Anil parkeri, also known as Indian Sarsaparilla, is a medicinal plant that belongs to the Aristolochiaceae family and is native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that can help treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Anil parkeri include alkaloids, saponins, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Anil parkeri is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and fever.
